#Text=Skater Boy
#Text=Sirius couldn’t do his homework.
#Text=What he used to do was go to the local cafe and order an iced tea.
#Text=It would keep him awake and the noises from all the people would keep him stimulated enough to focus.
#Text=As someone with ADHD, it was hard to find a place where he could concentrate, but he had finally found it there.
#Text=At least he used to be able to concentrate, until someone showed up who was making it very difficult.
#Text=This someone was a boy, maybe 6’3 and extremely lanky.
#Text=He dressed in large striped sweaters, usually tucked into a pair of ripped jeans cuffed on the ends.
#Text=He always wore Vans.
#Text=He must have only had one pair, because he only ever wore the originals and they were so old they were practically falling apart.
#Text=He had a mop of wavy golden brown hair threaded with auburn, short on the sizes and long on the top.
#Text=The boy was a skater, unsurprisingly.
#Text=He would casually skate around campus, doing various tricks as if it was the easiest thing in the world.
#Text=Sometimes he would gain quite an audience.
#Text=And, for whatever reason, he always had a red lollipop in his mouth.
#Text=And no matter how hard Sirius tried to study, he couldn’t when this skater was around.
#Text=Instead of focusing on his Art History assignment which was due tomorrow, Sirius was spending time thinking about how soft the boy’s hair must be.
#Text=It was so fluffy it was a crime that his hands weren’t threaded in it at that moment.
#Text=He shook his head, trying to bring his focus back to his essay.
#Text=Sirius ignored the snap of a skateboard being pushed against the ground and picked up because that only meant that the boy was leaving.
#Text=At least, that’s what he thought until he heard the very same skateboard being leaned against the table he was sitting at.
#Text=The noise made him start, nearly giving himself whiplash as he looked up.
#Text=He found himself staring into the skater’s amber eyes.
#Text=“Hello,” he grinned, his smile full of perfect white teeth.
#Text=“Mind if I sit here?
#Text=I’m desperate for a milkshake and all of the other tables are full.”
#Text=The words were spilling out of Sirius’ mouth.
#Text=“Yeah, of course.
#Text=As long as you like.”
#Text=“Thanks.”
#Text=The boy smiled his dazzling smile again and sat down on the chair opposite Sirius.
#Text=“My name’s Remus, by the way.”
#Text=Remus.
#Text=Sirius smiled to himself.
#Text=It suited him.
#Text=“Oh, uh - Sirius,” he added after a second too long.
#Text=“Sirius Black.”
#Text=“You come here a lot,” Remus said casually.
#Text=Sirius’ heart jumped.
#Text=Skater boy had been noticing him.
#Text=“You do too,” Sirius said.
#Text=He tried to stop there, but the word vomit was coming out of his mouth like an open faucet.
#Text=“You skate.
#Text=A lot.
#Text=And you always have a red lollipop in your mouth, which can’t be good for your health.
#Text=You also probably need to get another pair of Vans.”
#Text=He bit his lip suddenly.
#Text=Well, he’d done it - he’d gone and ruined another perfectly decent potential friendship.
#Text=Remus seemed unfazed, instead frowning slightly and looking under the table at his shoes.
#Text=“Alright, well you’re not wrong about the Vans.
#Text=These are the ones I learned to skate in.
#Text=I’m sentimental.
#Text=But you are wrong about the lollipop.”
#Text=Sirius knitted his eyebrows.
#Text=“No, I’m not.”
#Text=Remus pulled the lolly out of his mouth from where it was resting between his teeth with a quiet pop.
#Text=When Sirius saw it, he smiled.
#Text=“Sometimes it’s green, thank you very much,” Remus added, popping it back in his mouth.
#Text=“And like you can talk.
#Text=What about your obsessive ice tea drinking?
#Text=And how you always come here with your laptop or notebook but never write a word.
#Text=And you always wear that leather jacket.”
#Text=Sirius gaped.
#Text=He didn’t know what to say, but there was a warm feeling balling in the pit of his stomach.
#Text=“I-don’t…I have two leather jackets.”
#Text=Remus laughed.
#Text=“Fair enough.
#Text=But the lack of studying must be a serious problem for you.”
#Text=“I-I suppose,” Sirius said, shrugging and averting his eyes.
#Text=“Art History is hard.”
#Text=“Ah, I see,” Remus smirked.
#Text=“Well, I’m an English Lit major, but I do take an Art History class, so maybe I could help you out.”
#Text=Sirius looked up quickly to see Remus taking a pen out of his pocket and grabbing his hand from across the table.
#Text=Sirius’s heart fluttered.
#Text=Remus began writing something on his skin.
#Text=As soon as he was done, he stood up, grabbing his skateboard.
#Text=“Your milkshake,” Sirius said faintly.
#Text=Remus looked back, smiling.
#Text=He seemed to always be smiling.
#Text=“You must be clever enough to realise that that wasn’t the real reason I came over to you.”
#Text=He turned and got on his skateboard, flipping it over his feet as he left for good measure.
#Text=Sirius glanced down at his hand and saw a phone number and a smiley face scribbled.
#Text=He immediately typed it into his phone so he wouldn’t rub it off or lose it.
#Text=When he looked up again, he must have been grinning like an idiot.
#Text=He looked around the cafe, realising with a warm feeling in his stomach that there had been plenty of free tables.
#Text=Remus had chosen to sit there, and Sirius was ever so glad he did so.
